input.headline: Heimir Hallgrímsson assesses World Cup play-off draw

raw.headline: Heimir Hallgrímsson assesses World Cup play-off draw

winner: I hope Heimir's draw goes better than his last attempt at drawing a crowd—it was so bad, even the soccer players said they preferred penalty kicks!

candidates.text: The referee pulled out a tiny football and rolled it into an empty net before the teams even took their positions. "That," he said gravely, "is what I call a World Cup play-off draw.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the context of a World Cup play-off draw. It uses a clever visual metaphor with the tiny football, and the referee's gravely tone adds humor through contrast."}

candidates.text: Heimir Hallgrímsson drew the World Cup playoff like a child drawing mustache on Santa Claus—with enthusiasm and questionable taste.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative comparison with unexpected imagery, good specificity and humor"}

candidates.text: Heimir Hallgrímsson’s assessment was simple: "The play-offs are going exactly as expected—except for that one goal no one saw coming.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific to the context of a World Cup play-off draw, and includes a surprising twist with the 'one goal no one saw coming,' which adds humor while staying natural and relevant."}

candidates.text: I hope Heimir's draw goes better than his last attempt at drawing a crowd—it was so bad, even the soccer players said they preferred penalty kicks!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and uses a play on 'drawing a crowd' versus the soccer context, but the humor feels forced and the reference to 'last attempt at drawing a crowd' lacks clear specificity or context."}

candidates.text: Heimir Hallgrímsson's draw wasn't just for soccer fans—it was a full-blown *penalty* of wit!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'penalty,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity about Heimir Hallgrímsson or the World Cup play-off draw."}
